title: Emitter — bump version 1→2 + rename payload.narrow → payload.entries
description: Mechanical rename + version bump on the producer side. Changes the TypeScript type, the runtime emission, and the docstring example. NO compat shim, NO accept-both. After this commit, the CLI emits `version: 2` with `entries` and never emits `narrow` again.

files.change: (line 18 docstring) `version: 1,` → `version: 2,`; (line 24) rename docstring field `narrow:` → `entries:`; (line 49-54) rename interface `PlanContextHintNarrowEntry` → `PlanContextHintEntry`; (line 56-62) rename type field `narrow` → `entries`, change `version: 1` → `version: 2`; (line 157) rename local `const narrow:` → `const entries:`; (line 165) emit `version: 2`; (line 168) emit `entries,` instead of `narrow,`; add a 4-line type-comment above the interface declaring `// Protocol v2 (rc.18): renamed `narrow` → `entries` (field is mode-agnostic — covers both --paths union and --all degenerate); v1 emission is removed without a compat shim per pre-user clean-slate policy.`

implementation: Open packages/cli/src/commands/plan-context-hint.ts; locate the docstring block at lines 16-32 and update the inline JSON example to use `version: 2` and `entries:` (preserving indentation and comment alignment)
Rename the exported interface `PlanContextHintNarrowEntry` → `PlanContextHintEntry` (single declaration site at line 49 — TS compiler will surface any importers to fix in the same commit)
Update `PlanContextHintOutput` type: change `version: 1` → `version: 2` (line 57); rename field `narrow: PlanContextHintNarrowEntry[]` → `entries: PlanContextHintEntry[]` (line 60)

Update `runPlanContextHint`: rename local `const narrow: PlanContextHintNarrowEntry[] = ...` → `const entries: PlanContextHintEntry[] = ...` at line 157; in the return object at lines 164-170 update both `version: 2` and `entries,` (drop the `narrow,` shorthand)
Add the 4-line type-comment block above the `PlanContextHintOutput` interface declaring the v2 cut and citing pre-user clean-slate policy
Run `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli build` (or `pnpm tsc -b`) and confirm zero TypeScript errors — any importer of the old interface name surfaces here
Commit message: `feat(rc18)!: bump plan-context-hint protocol to v2 — rename payload.narrow → payload.entries`

reference.pattern: Mechanical rename — interface declaration site is single, TS compiler enforces lockstep
reference.examples: Same shape as any prior protocol-bump precedent in the repo (search `git log --all --oneline | grep -i 'protocol\|version'` if you need a reference commit)

criteria: Exported interface renamed `PlanContextHintNarrowEntry` → `PlanContextHintEntry` (zero remaining references to the old name)

Wire shape: `version: 2` + `entries: PlanContextHintEntry[]` (zero remaining references to `narrow` as a TYPE FIELD — local helper-function arg names that happen to be `narrow` are out of scope unless they leak)

Test command: `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli build && grep -c 'version: 2' packages/cli/src/commands/plan-context-hint.ts | grep -q '^[2-9]' && ! grep -E '(version: 1|payload\.narrow|narrow:)' packages/cli/src/commands/plan-context-hint.ts`
Behavioral expectation: TypeScript build passes; running the (not-yet-updated) tests should now FAIL with field-name mismatch — this proves the wire change took effect

Field-name consistency check: emitter emits `entries`, not `narrow` (verified via `grep -c 'entries' packages/cli/src/commands/plan-context-hint.ts` ≥ 5 matches: type field, runtime emission, docstring, local var, comment)
Version-receipt stance check: NOT applicable to this task (emitter only); stance is enforced in TASK-003/004
Single git commit recorded with breaking-change marker (`!`) in conventional commits format

rationale.chosen_approach: Land the emitter change first (in any execution order — parallel-safe with TASK-003/004 since they touch different files); the test failure post-emitter is a desired signal that downstream lockstep is needed
alternatives_considered: Land tests first to drive the rename
Bundle emitter + 2 consumers + tests in a single commit
decision_factors: Parallel-safe execution (TASK-002/003/004 fork the same parent)
Atomic per-file changes are easier to review and revert
rationale.tradeoffs: Mid-chain CI may briefly fail if commits are pushed individually before TASK-005 lands — acceptable since rc.18 ships SOLO and the commit chain is local until merge

manual_checks: Inspect the emitted JSON shape by running `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli build && node packages/cli/dist/index.js plan-context-hint --all --target $(pwd)` and confirming the printed object has top-level `version: 2` and `entries: [...]`, no `narrow` key
Confirm no remaining `version: 1` literal in the emitter source
